title: Ixora lanceolata
taxonomy: Ixora lanceolata is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to South Asia and Southeast Asia, and is commonly known as the lance-leaved jessamine or lance-leaved ixora. The genus Ixora is a group of flowering plants that are typically found in tropical and subtropical regions. Ixora lanceolata is a shrub that grows up to 3 meters in height, with oval-shaped leaves and funnel-shaped flowers that are typically red or pink in color. The plant is often used as an ornamental in gardens and landscapes due to its attractive flowers and compact growth habit.

The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. intro: Ixora lanceolata, also known as Jungle Geranium, is a plant species native to tropical regions of Asia, characterized by its showy, pink to purple flowers and lance-shaped leaves. The health benefits of Ixora lanceolata include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable traditional medicine in various cultures.
